content.claim: Systematic review of rural depopulation policy explicitly notes that almost all literature covers only European regions and that all reviewed policies target depopulation reduction as a quantitative variable, but no study addresses qualitative aspects of depopulation reduction, limiting scalability and validation for non-European contexts
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Caveat itself comes from a review of European literature and does not resolve the limitation for Hungarian-specific application

No Hungarian-specific study has validated the closure→depopulation causal logic against Hungary's pre-existing settlement structure (many very small, ethnically Roma-concentrated villages) or post-2013 centralized financing

Hungarian context is entangled with segregation and poverty dynamics (catchment policies, residential mobility toward advantaged localities) not equally salient in Scottish Highlands or French Alpine cases, further constraining mechanism transferability
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
